 {"id":"caam-qa1","title":"Add --browser flag to profile add command","description":"## Task
Extend 'caam profile add' to accept browser configuration during profile creation.

## New Flags
- --browser: Browser type (chrome, firefox, default)
- --browser-profile: Browser profile name or directory

## Example Usage
```bash
caam profile add codex work --browser chrome --browser-profile 'Profile 2'
caam profile add claude personal --browser firefox --browser-profile 'personal-firefox'
```

## Implementation
- Add flags to profileAddCmd in cmd/caam/cmd/root.go
- Pass values to profile.Create()
- Store in profile.json

## UX Consideration
Could also support interactive browser selection if flags not provided. List detected Chrome profiles and let user choose.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Flags accepted and stored in profile
- Profile loads correctly with browser config
- Works with existing profiles (backwards compatible)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T00:50:51.770335947-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T01:21:48.713708067-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T01:21:48.713708067-05:00","close_reason":"Added --browser, --browser-profile, and --browser-name flags to 'profile add' command. Profile status now shows browser config.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-qa1","depends_on_id":"caam-4eg","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-17T00:50:51.784339072-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"caam-qa1","depends_on_id":"caam-ypx","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T00:50:51.785523973-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"caam-qpx","title":"Set up Bubble Tea scaffolding and main model","description":"## Task
Create the foundational TUI code structure using Bubble Tea.

## Files to Create
- internal/ui/model.go - Main application model
- internal/ui/styles.go - Lipgloss styles
- internal/ui/keys.go - Keybindings

## Main Model Structure
```go
type Model struct {
    providers    []string           // codex, claude, gemini
    activeProvider int              // Currently selected provider
    profiles     map[string][]*profile.Profile
    selected     int                // Currently selected profile
    width, height int               // Terminal dimensions
    state        viewState          // list, detail, confirm, etc.
    err          error
}
```

## Implementation Notes
- Implement tea.Model interface (Init, Update, View)
- Handle window resize events
- Set up basic layout (will be refined in panel tasks)
- Add dependency: go get github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea

## Acceptance Criteria
- TUI launches with 'caam' (no args)
- Basic model structure in place
- Handles resize events
- Can quit with 'q'","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T00:51:43.546283097-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T01:26:57.115215337-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T01:26:57.115215337-05:00","close_reason":"TUI scaffolding complete: model.go, styles.go, keys.go implemented. TUI launches with 'caam' (no args), handles resize, quit with 'q'. Build verified.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-qpx","depends_on_id":"caam-by4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-17T00:51:43.566635847-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

-{"id":"caam-r36","title":"Activity event logging system","description":"## Purpose
Create a system to log all caam events (activations, logins, errors, switches) for analytics.

## Background
codex-pool tracks per-request logs. Since caam does not proxy requests, we track what
caam controls: profile activations, login events, errors, and profile switches.

## Event Types
```go
const (
    EventActivate  = "activate"   // Profile activated
    EventLogin     = "login"      // User logged in
    EventRefresh   = "refresh"    // Token refreshed
    EventError     = "error"      // Error occurred
    EventSwitch    = "switch"     // Switched from one profile to another
    EventDeactivate = "deactivate" // Profile deactivated
)
```

## Technical Implementation

### Event Logger Interface
```go
// internal/db/events.go
type Event struct {
    Timestamp   time.Time
    Type        string
    Provider    string
    ProfileName string
    Details     map[string]interface{}
    Duration    time.Duration // For session tracking
}

type EventLogger interface {
    Log(event Event) error
    GetEvents(provider, profile string, since time.Time, limit int) ([]Event, error)
    GetStats(provider, profile string) (*ProfileStats, error)
}

// Atomic logging with transaction
func (d *DB) LogEvent(event Event) error {
    tx, err := d.conn.Begin()
    if err != nil {
        return err
    }
    defer tx.Rollback()
    
    // Insert into activity_log
    _, err = tx.Exec(`INSERT INTO activity_log (...) VALUES (...)`, ...)
    if err != nil {
        return err
    }
    
    // Update profile_stats atomically
    _, err = tx.Exec(`
        INSERT INTO profile_stats (provider, profile_name, total_activations)
        VALUES (?, ?, 1)
        ON CONFLICT(provider, profile_name) DO UPDATE SET
            total_activations = total_activations + 1,
            last_activated = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
    `, event.Provider, event.ProfileName)
    
    return tx.Commit()
}
```

### Session Tracking
Track active sessions to calculate duration:
```go
// Store session start in memory, write duration on deactivate
type SessionTracker struct {
    mu       sync.Mutex
    sessions map[string]time.Time // key: "provider/profile"
}

func (s *SessionTracker) Start(provider, profile string)
func (s *SessionTracker) End(provider, profile string) time.Duration
```

## Integration Points
- Activate command logs EventActivate
- Login command logs EventLogin
- Token refresh logs EventRefresh
- Errors log EventError
- Profile switch logs EventSwitch + EventActivate

## Files to Create
- internal/db/events.go
- internal/db/events_test.go
- internal/db/session.go

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All event types are loggable
- [ ] Profile stats are updated atomically
- [ ] Session duration is tracked accurately
- [ ] Events can be queried by provider/profile/time range
- [ ] Handles concurrent logging safely","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T16:22:31.646786371-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T16:22:31.646786371-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-r36","depends_on_id":"caam-fwl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T16:28:51.960082168-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
+{"id":"caam-r36","title":"Activity event logging system","description":"## Purpose
Create a system to log all caam events (activations, logins, errors, switches) for analytics.

## Background
codex-pool tracks per-request logs. Since caam does not proxy requests, we track what
caam controls: profile activations, login events, errors, and profile switches.

## Event Types
```go
const (
    EventActivate  = "activate"   // Profile activated
    EventLogin     = "login"      // User logged in
    EventRefresh   = "refresh"    // Token refreshed
    EventError     = "error"      // Error occurred
    EventSwitch    = "switch"     // Switched from one profile to another
    EventDeactivate = "deactivate" // Profile deactivated
)
```

## Technical Implementation

### Event Logger Interface
```go
// internal/db/events.go
type Event struct {
    Timestamp   time.Time
    Type        string
    Provider    string
    ProfileName string
    Details     map[string]interface{}
    Duration    time.Duration // For session tracking
}

type EventLogger interface {
    Log(event Event) error
    GetEvents(provider, profile string, since time.Time, limit int) ([]Event, error)
    GetStats(provider, profile string) (*ProfileStats, error)
}

// Atomic logging with transaction
func (d *DB) LogEvent(event Event) error {
    tx, err := d.conn.Begin()
    if err != nil {
        return err
    }
    defer tx.Rollback()
    
    // Insert into activity_log
    _, err = tx.Exec(`INSERT INTO activity_log (...) VALUES (...)`, ...)
    if err != nil {
        return err
    }
    
    // Update profile_stats atomically
    _, err = tx.Exec(`
        INSERT INTO profile_stats (provider, profile_name, total_activations)
        VALUES (?, ?, 1)
        ON CONFLICT(provider, profile_name) DO UPDATE SET
            total_activations = total_activations + 1,
            last_activated = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
    `, event.Provider, event.ProfileName)
    
    return tx.Commit()
}
```

### Session Tracking
Track active sessions to calculate duration:
```go
// Store session start in memory, write duration on deactivate
type SessionTracker struct {
    mu       sync.Mutex
    sessions map[string]time.Time // key: "provider/profile"
}

func (s *SessionTracker) Start(provider, profile string)
func (s *SessionTracker) End(provider, profile string) time.Duration
```

## Integration Points

- Login command logs EventLogin
- Token refresh logs EventRefresh
- Errors log EventError
- Profile switch logs EventSwitch + EventActivate

- internal/db/session.go

- [ ] Profile stats are updated atomically
- [ ] Session duration is tracked accurately
- [ ] Events can be queried by provider/profile/time range
- [ ] Handles concurrent logging safely","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T16:22:31.646786371-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T20:03:13.605561636-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T20:03:13.605561636-05:00","close_reason":"Added DB-backed activity logging APIs, stats updates, session tracking, and tests.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-r36","depends_on_id":"caam-fwl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T16:28:51.960082168-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":19,"issue_id":"caam-r36","author":"ubuntu","text":"Starting activity event logging on top of internal/db: add Event types + DB.LogEvent/GetEvents/GetStats and SessionTracker for duration.  {"id":"caam-thb","title":"Token expiry parsing for all providers","description":"# Token Expiry Parsing

## Purpose
Parse token expiration timestamps from each provider authentication files to determine how long tokens remain valid.

## Background
Each provider stores auth differently. We need to extract expiry info from:
1. Claude Code - oauth.json with expiry field
2. OpenAI Codex - auth.json with different structure
3. Google Gemini - gcloud ADC format

## Technical Requirements

### Parser Interface
```go
type ExpiryParser interface {
    ParseExpiry(authFilePath string) (time.Time, error)
}

func ParseClaudeExpiry(path string) (time.Time, error)
func ParseCodexExpiry(path string) (time.Time, error)
func ParseGeminiExpiry(path string) (time.Time, error)
```

### Provider-Specific Formats

#### Claude Code
```json
// ~/.claude.ai/oauth.json (example, needs verification)
{
  "access_token": "...",
  "refresh_token": "...",
  "expires_at": "2025-12-17T18:00:00Z",
  "token_type": "Bearer"
}
```
Alternative: `expires_in` (seconds) + issued time

#### OpenAI Codex
```json
// ~/.codex/auth.json (example, needs verification)
{
  "access_token": "...",
  "refresh_token": "...",
  "expires_at": 1734451200,  // Unix timestamp
  "token_type": "Bearer"
}
```

#### Google Gemini (ADC)
```json
// ~/.config/gcloud/application_default_credentials.json
{
  "client_id": "...",
  "client_secret": "...",
  "refresh_token": "...",
  "type": "authorized_user"
}
```
Note: ADC may not store expiry - needs refresh to get expiry.

### Fallback Strategy
If expiry cannot be determined:
1. Check if refresh_token exists → assume valid but unknown expiry
2. No refresh_token → mark as "needs login"
3. Return sentinel value for "unknown expiry"

## Implementation Notes
- Study actual auth file formats (may need reverse engineering)
- Handle missing files gracefully
- Handle malformed JSON
- Support both Unix timestamps and ISO8601

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] ParseClaudeExpiry implemented and tested
- [ ] ParseCodexExpiry implemented and tested  
- [ ] ParseGeminiExpiry implemented and tested
- [ ] Handles missing files (returns error)
- [ ] Handles malformed JSON (returns error)
- [ ] Unit tests with fixture files

## Files to Create/Modify
- `internal/health/expiry.go` (new)
- `internal/health/expiry_test.go` (new)
- `internal/health/testdata/` (fixtures)

## Research Needed
- [ ] Verify Claude Code auth file format
- [ ] Verify Codex auth file format
- [ ] Determine if Gemini ADC includes expiry

## Dependencies
None","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T16:17:51.23607834-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T16:44:20.264399828-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T16:44:20.264399828-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented token expiry parsing for Claude, Codex, and Gemini providers. Supports ISO8601, RFC3339, Unix timestamps. Includes ExpiryInfo struct with TTL(), IsExpired(), NeedsRefresh() methods. All tests pass.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-thb","depends_on_id":"caam-6gj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T16:27:17.106895472-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
